title: salix × fragilis f. sanguinea
intro: Salix × fragilis f. sanguinea, also known as the 'Weeping Willow', is a hybrid plant species derived from the weeping willow (Salix × fragilis) and the goosely willow (Salix × sanguinea). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix × fragilis f. sanguinea include its ability to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote wound healing. Salix × fragilis f. sanguinea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various ailments.
